speaker: ROMEO
text: He jests at scars that never felt a wound.

speaker: ROMEO
text: But soft, what light through yonder window breaks?

speaker: ROMEO
text: It is the East, and Juliet is the sun.

speaker: ROMEO
text: Arise, fair sun, and kill the envious moon,

speaker: ROMEO
text: Who is already sick and pale with grief

speaker: ROMEO
text: That thou, her maid, art far more fair than she.

speaker: ROMEO
text: Be not her maid since she is envious.

speaker: ROMEO
text: Her vestal livery is but sick and green,

speaker: ROMEO
text: And none but fools do wear it. Cast it off.

speaker: ROMEO
text: It is my lady. O, it is my love!

speaker: ROMEO
text: O, that she knew she were!

speaker: ROMEO
text: She speaks, yet she says nothing. What of that?

speaker: ROMEO
text: Her eye discourses; I will answer it.

speaker: ROMEO
text: I am too bold. ’Tis not to me she speaks.

speaker: ROMEO
text: Two of the fairest stars in all the heaven,

speaker: ROMEO
text: Having some business, do entreat her eyes

speaker: ROMEO
text: To twinkle in their spheres till they return.

speaker: ROMEO
text: What if her eyes were there, they in her head?

speaker: ROMEO
text: The brightness of her cheek would shame those stars

speaker: ROMEO
text: As daylight doth a lamp; her eye in heaven

speaker: ROMEO
text: Would through the airy region stream so bright

speaker: ROMEO
text: That birds would sing and think it were not night.

speaker: ROMEO
text: See how she leans her cheek upon her hand.

speaker: ROMEO
text: O, that I were a glove upon that hand,

speaker: ROMEO
text: That I might touch that cheek!

speaker: JULIET
text: Ay me.

speaker: ROMEO
text: She speaks.

speaker: ROMEO
text: O, speak again, bright angel, for thou art

speaker: ROMEO
text: As glorious to this night, being o’er my head,

speaker: ROMEO
text: As is a wingèd messenger of heaven

speaker: ROMEO
text: Unto the white-upturnèd wond’ring eyes

speaker: ROMEO
text: Of mortals that fall back to gaze on him

speaker: ROMEO
text: When he bestrides the lazy puffing clouds

speaker: ROMEO
text: And sails upon the bosom of the air.

speaker: JULIET
text: O Romeo, Romeo, wherefore art thou Romeo?

speaker: JULIET
text: Deny thy father and refuse thy name,

speaker: JULIET
text: Or, if thou wilt not, be but sworn my love,

speaker: JULIET
text: And I’ll no longer be a Capulet.

speaker: ROMEO
text: Shall I hear more, or shall I speak at this?

speaker: JULIET
text: ’Tis but thy name that is my enemy.

speaker: JULIET
text: Thou art thyself, though not a Montague.

speaker: JULIET
text: What’s Montague? It is nor hand, nor foot,

speaker: JULIET
text: Nor arm, nor face. O, be some other name

speaker: JULIET
text: Belonging to a man.

speaker: JULIET
text: What’s in a name? That which we call a rose

speaker: JULIET
text: By any other word would smell as sweet.

speaker: JULIET
text: So Romeo would, were he not Romeo called,

speaker: JULIET
text: Retain that dear perfection which he owes

speaker: JULIET
text: Without that title. Romeo, doff thy name,

speaker: JULIET
text: And, for thy name, which is no part of thee,

speaker: JULIET
text: Take all myself.

speaker: ROMEO
text: I take thee at thy word.

speaker: ROMEO
text: Call me but love, and I’ll be new baptized.

speaker: ROMEO
text: Henceforth I never will be Romeo.

speaker: JULIET
text: What man art thou that, thus bescreened in night,

speaker: JULIET
text: So stumblest on my counsel?

speaker: ROMEO
text: By a name

speaker: ROMEO
text: I know not how to tell thee who I am.

speaker: ROMEO
text: My name, dear saint, is hateful to myself

speaker: ROMEO
text: Because it is an enemy to thee.

speaker: ROMEO
text: Had I it written, I would tear the word.

speaker: JULIET
text: My ears have yet not drunk a hundred words

speaker: JULIET
text: Of thy tongue’s uttering, yet I know the sound.

speaker: JULIET
text: Art thou not Romeo, and a Montague?

speaker: ROMEO
text: Neither, fair maid, if either thee dislike.

speaker: JULIET
text: How camest thou hither, tell me, and wherefore?

speaker: JULIET
text: The orchard walls are high and hard to climb,

speaker: JULIET
text: And the place death, considering who thou art,

speaker: JULIET
text: If any of my kinsmen find thee here.

speaker: ROMEO
text: With love’s light wings did I o’erperch these walls,

speaker: ROMEO
text: For stony limits cannot hold love out,

speaker: ROMEO
text: And what love can do, that dares love attempt.

speaker: ROMEO
text: Therefore thy kinsmen are no stop to me.

speaker: JULIET
text: If they do see thee, they will murder thee.

speaker: ROMEO
text: Alack, there lies more peril in thine eye

speaker: ROMEO
text: Than twenty of their swords. Look thou but sweet,

speaker: ROMEO
text: And I am proof against their enmity.

speaker: JULIET
text: I would not for the world they saw thee here.

speaker: ROMEO
text: I have night’s cloak to hide me from their eyes,

speaker: ROMEO
text: And, but thou love me, let them find me here.

speaker: ROMEO
text: My life were better ended by their hate

speaker: ROMEO
text: Than death proroguèd, wanting of thy love.

speaker: JULIET
text: By whose direction found’st thou out this place?

speaker: ROMEO
text: By love, that first did prompt me to inquire.

speaker: ROMEO
text: He lent me counsel, and I lent him eyes.

speaker: ROMEO
text: I am no pilot; yet, wert thou as far

speaker: ROMEO
text: As that vast shore washed with the farthest sea,

speaker: ROMEO
text: I should adventure for such merchandise.

speaker: JULIET
text: Thou knowest the mask of night is on my face,

speaker: JULIET
text: Else would a maiden blush bepaint my cheek

speaker: JULIET
text: For that which thou hast heard me speak tonight.

speaker: JULIET
text: Fain would I dwell on form; fain, fain deny

speaker: JULIET
text: What I have spoke. But farewell compliment.

speaker: JULIET
text: Dost thou love me? I know thou wilt say “Ay,”

speaker: JULIET
text: And I will take thy word. Yet, if thou swear’st,

speaker: JULIET
text: Thou mayst prove false. At lovers’ perjuries,

speaker: JULIET
text: They say, Jove laughs. O gentle Romeo,

speaker: JULIET
text: If thou dost love, pronounce it faithfully.

speaker: JULIET
text: Or, if thou thinkest I am too quickly won,

speaker: JULIET
text: I’ll frown and be perverse and say thee nay,

speaker: JULIET
text: So thou wilt woo, but else not for the world.

speaker: JULIET
text: In truth, fair Montague, I am too fond,

speaker: JULIET
text: And therefore thou mayst think my havior light.

speaker: JULIET
text: But trust me, gentleman, I’ll prove more true

speaker: JULIET
text: Than those that have more coying to be strange.

speaker: JULIET
text: I should have been more strange, I must confess,

speaker: JULIET
text: But that thou overheard’st ere I was ware

speaker: JULIET
text: My true-love passion. Therefore pardon me,

speaker: JULIET
text: And not impute this yielding to light love,

speaker: JULIET
text: Which the dark night hath so discoverèd.

speaker: ROMEO
text: Lady, by yonder blessèd moon I vow,

speaker: ROMEO
text: That tips with silver all these fruit-tree tops—

speaker: JULIET
text: O, swear not by the moon, th’ inconstant moon,

speaker: JULIET
text: That monthly changes in her circled orb,

speaker: JULIET
text: Lest that thy love prove likewise variable.

speaker: ROMEO
text: What shall I swear by?

speaker: JULIET
text: Do not swear at all.

speaker: JULIET
text: Or, if thou wilt, swear by thy gracious self,

speaker: JULIET
text: Which is the god of my idolatry,

speaker: JULIET
text: And I’ll believe thee.

speaker: ROMEO
text: If my heart’s dear love—

speaker: JULIET
text: Well, do not swear. Although I joy in thee,

speaker: JULIET
text: I have no joy of this contract tonight.

speaker: JULIET
text: It is too rash, too unadvised, too sudden,

speaker: JULIET
text: Too like the lightning, which doth cease to be

speaker: JULIET
text: Ere one can say “It lightens.” Sweet, good night.

speaker: JULIET
text: This bud of love, by summer’s ripening breath,

speaker: JULIET
text: May prove a beauteous flower when next we meet.

speaker: JULIET
text: Good night, good night. As sweet repose and rest

speaker: JULIET
text: Come to thy heart as that within my breast.

speaker: ROMEO
text: O, wilt thou leave me so unsatisfied?

speaker: JULIET
text: What satisfaction canst thou have tonight?

speaker: ROMEO
text: Th’ exchange of thy love’s faithful vow for mine.

speaker: JULIET
text: I gave thee mine before thou didst request it,

speaker: JULIET
text: And yet I would it were to give again.

speaker: ROMEO
text: Wouldst thou withdraw it? For what purpose, love?

speaker: JULIET
text: But to be frank and give it thee again.

speaker: JULIET
text: And yet I wish but for the thing I have.

speaker: JULIET
text: My bounty is as boundless as the sea,

speaker: JULIET
text: My love as deep. The more I give to thee,

speaker: JULIET
text: The more I have, for both are infinite.

speaker: JULIET
text: I hear some noise within. Dear love, adieu.—

speaker: JULIET
text: Anon, good nurse.—Sweet Montague, be true.

speaker: JULIET
text: Stay but a little; I will come again.

speaker: ROMEO
text: O blessèd, blessèd night! I am afeard,

speaker: ROMEO
text: Being in night, all this is but a dream,

speaker: ROMEO
text: Too flattering sweet to be substantial.

speaker: JULIET
text: Three words, dear Romeo, and good night indeed.

speaker: JULIET
text: If that thy bent of love be honorable,

speaker: JULIET
text: Thy purpose marriage, send me word tomorrow,

speaker: JULIET
text: By one that I’ll procure to come to thee,

speaker: JULIET
text: Where and what time thou wilt perform the rite,

speaker: JULIET
text: And all my fortunes at thy foot I’ll lay

speaker: JULIET
text: And follow thee my lord throughout the world.

speaker: NURSE
text: Madam.

speaker: JULIET
text: I come anon.—But if thou meanest not well,

speaker: JULIET
text: I do beseech thee—

speaker: NURSE
text: Madam.

speaker: JULIET
text: By and by, I come.—

speaker: JULIET
text: To cease thy strife and leave me to my grief.

speaker: JULIET
text: Tomorrow will I send.

speaker: ROMEO
text: So thrive my soul—

speaker: JULIET
text: A thousand times good night.

speaker: ROMEO
text: A thousand times the worse to want thy light.

speaker: ROMEO
text: Love goes toward love as schoolboys from their books,

speaker: ROMEO
text: But love from love, toward school with heavy looks.

speaker: JULIET
text: Hist, Romeo, hist! O, for a falc’ner’s voice

speaker: JULIET
text: To lure this tassel-gentle back again!

speaker: JULIET
text: Bondage is hoarse and may not speak aloud,

speaker: JULIET
text: Else would I tear the cave where Echo lies

speaker: JULIET
text: And make her airy tongue more hoarse than mine

speaker: JULIET
text: With repetition of “My Romeo!”

speaker: ROMEO
text: It is my soul that calls upon my name.

speaker: ROMEO
text: How silver-sweet sound lovers’ tongues by night,

speaker: ROMEO
text: Like softest music to attending ears.

speaker: JULIET
text: Romeo.

speaker: ROMEO
text: My dear.

speaker: JULIET
text: What o’clock tomorrow

speaker: JULIET
text: Shall I send to thee?

speaker: ROMEO
text: By the hour of nine.

speaker: JULIET
text: I will not fail. ’Tis twenty year till then.

speaker: JULIET
text: I have forgot why I did call thee back.

speaker: ROMEO
text: Let me stand here till thou remember it.

speaker: JULIET
text: I shall forget, to have thee still stand there,

speaker: JULIET
text: Rememb’ring how I love thy company.

speaker: ROMEO
text: And I’ll still stay, to have thee still forget,

speaker: ROMEO
text: Forgetting any other home but this.

speaker: JULIET
text: ’Tis almost morning. I would have thee gone,

speaker: JULIET
text: And yet no farther than a wanton’s bird,

speaker: JULIET
text: That lets it hop a little from his hand,

speaker: JULIET
text: Like a poor prisoner in his twisted gyves,

speaker: JULIET
text: And with a silken thread plucks it back again,

speaker: JULIET
text: So loving-jealous of his liberty.

speaker: ROMEO
text: I would I were thy bird.

speaker: JULIET
text: Sweet, so would I.

speaker: JULIET
text: Yet I should kill thee with much cherishing.

speaker: JULIET
text: Good night, good night. Parting is such sweet sorrow

speaker: JULIET
text: That I shall say “Good night” till it be morrow.

speaker: ROMEO
text: Sleep dwell upon thine eyes, peace in thy breast.

speaker: ROMEO
text: Would I were sleep and peace so sweet to rest.

speaker: ROMEO
text: Hence will I to my ghostly friar’s close cell,

speaker: ROMEO
text: His help to crave, and my dear hap to tell.
